children.heading: .—Appropriate congressional committees
children.label: 1 .—Appropriate congressional committees
children.text: The term “appropriate congressional committees” means the Committee on Transportation and Infrastructure of the House of Representatives and the Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation of the Senate.

children.heading: .—Chief acquisition officer
children.label: 2 .—Chief acquisition officer
children.text: The term “Chief Acquisition Officer” means the officer appointed under .section 308 of this title

children.heading: .—Customer of a major acquisition program
children.label: 3 .—Customer of a major acquisition program
children.text: The term “customer of a major acquisition program” means the operating field unit of the Coast Guard that will field the system or systems acquired under a major acquisition program.

children.label: i
children.text: the estimated life-cycle costs of which exceed $1,000,000,000; or

children.label: ii
children.text: the estimated total acquisition costs of which exceed $300,000,000; or

children.label: I
children.text: the experimental or technically immature nature of the asset;

children.label: II
children.text: the technological complexity of the asset;

children.label: III
children.text: the commitment of resources; or

children.label: IV
children.text: the nature of the capability or set of capabilities to be achieved; or

children.label: ii
children.text: because such acquisition is a joint acquisition.

children.label: A
children.text: the estimated life-cycle costs of which are equal to or less than $1,000,000,000, but greater than $300,000,000; or

children.label: B
children.text: the estimated total acquisition costs of which are equal to or less than $300,000,000, but greater than $100,000,000.

children.heading: .—Life-cycle cost
children.label: 6 .—Life-cycle cost
children.text: The term “life-cycle cost” means all costs for development, procurement, construction, and operations and support for a particular capability or asset, without regard to funding source or management control.

children.heading: .—Major acquisition program
children.label: 7 .—Major acquisition program
children.text: The term “major acquisition program” means an ongoing acquisition undertaken by the Coast Guard with a life-cycle cost estimate greater than or equal to $300,000,000.

children.label: A
children.text: to develop, produce, and deploy a new asset to meet identified operational requirements; and

children.label: B
children.text: to manage cost, schedule, and performance of the acquisition, project, or program.

children.heading: .—Safety concern
children.label: 9 .—Safety concern
children.text: The term “safety concern” means any hazard associated with a capability or asset or a subsystem of a capability or asset that is likely to cause serious bodily injury or death to a typical Coast Guard user in testing, maintaining, repairing, or operating the capability, asset, or subsystem or any hazard associated with the capability, asset, or subsystem that is likely to cause major damage to the capability, asset, or subsystem during the course of its normal operation by a typical Coast Guard user.

children.label: A
children.text: the testing of a capability or asset and the subsystems of the capability or asset to determine whether they meet all contractual performance requirements, including technical performance requirements, supportability requirements, and interoperability requirements and related specifications; and

children.label: B
children.text: the evaluation of the results of such testing.

children.label: A
children.text: the testing of a capability or asset and the subsystems of the capability or asset, under conditions similar to those in which the capability or asset and subsystems will actually be deployed, for the purpose of determining the effectiveness and suitability of the capability or asset and subsystems for use by typical Coast Guard users to conduct those missions for which the capability or asset and subsystems are intended to be used; and

children.label: B
children.text: the evaluation of the results of such testing.
